mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is structurally elevated due to demographic shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand and wealth effects from the housing market, which amplifies corporate earnings growth and produces positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when housing starts are driven by speculative bubbles rather than fundamental demand, evidenced by negative forward returns in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, an accelerating industrial production index reflects improved capacity utilization and supply-side responsiveness, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, or 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220, it indicates accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of periods above the threshold, or the inflation signal is neutralized by simultaneous wage growth that sustains consumption.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal loses predictive power when payroll growth is concentrated in low-productivity sectors, evidenced by flat or negative forward returns despite favorable labor metrics.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >50% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the correlation coefficient between GDP growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.15 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which precedes negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >60% of double-dip contraction periods, or the signal is consistently overridden by aggressive monetary policy interventions.
